title: Quantization-Aware Warmdown
research_question: Can the last part of training make the final int8+zlib submission behave better after quantization, even if the raw fp training objective looks similar?

candidates.name: o1-longer-tail
candidates.goal: Make the end of training decay more gently before export.
candidates.hypothesis: A longer warmdown may leave weights in a smoother, more quantization-friendly state without hurting the final compressed score.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the compressed model wants a gentler finish than the fp model.
candidates.why_worthy: This is directly tied to the challenge artifact we actually submit, not generic training aesthetics.
candidates.description: extend the final learning-rate decay tail before export

candidates.name: o2-early-warmdown
candidates.goal: Start the warmdown earlier to test a more conservative finish.
candidates.hypothesis: The current schedule may stay too hot too long for the compressed model, and an earlier tail could improve post-quantization behavior.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether warmdown timing matters more than warmdown length.
candidates.why_worthy: This tests a different scheduling mechanism from simply lengthening the tail.
candidates.description: start the final decay earlier with the same overall 600s budget

candidates.name: o3-final-stabilize
candidates.goal: Insert a short low-LR stabilization phase right before export.
candidates.hypothesis: A brief cooldown at the end may reduce quantization damage even if the raw fp model changes very little.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether a dedicated export-stabilization phase is better than a smoother global schedule.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the most challenge-specific schedule idea in the tranche.
candidates.description: add a short low-LR stabilization phase before serialization

candidates.name: o4-head-only-cooldown
candidates.goal: Stabilize the output path more than the backbone before export.
candidates.hypothesis: The output head may be especially sensitive to quantization, so cooling it more than the rest of the model could improve compressed behavior.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether post-quantization fragility is concentrated in the output path.
candidates.why_worthy: Output path has already proven to be first-class in this project, so it is a plausible place for quantization fragility to live.
candidates.description: apply a stronger final cooldown to the output head than to the backbone

candidates.name: o5-two-phase-quant-tail
candidates.goal: Combine a smoother warmdown with a final stabilization phase.
candidates.hypothesis: If quantization-friendly finishing is real, the best version may need both a gentler tail and a short stabilization window.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the schedule effects stack or whether only one tail mechanism matters.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the tranche's justified combo run because the whole point is end-of-training interaction effects.
candidates.description: combine a longer warmdown and a short final stabilization phase
